description: A long soft-bodied animal without limbs can be called 'muriwe'. An earthworm is the usual reading when context supplies no narrower kind. Worm is an everyday shape category rather than one biological group, and many of its members never touch soil. Species and medical classifications keep their precise names.
articulatory_notes: The lips meet for nasal 'm' and open into high-back rounded 'u'. A tap or brief trill leads into stressed high-front 'i'; rounded 'w' then glides forward into final 'e'.
usage_notes: A counted worm may take optional living classifier 'lipha'. 'muila muriwe' specifies an earthworm or soil worm when place supplies the intended distinction.

semantic_domains.nature: an everyday category for long soft-bodied animals without limbs
